title: RVT.REF.001.BENEFICIARY_REFERENTIAL_UPDATED — A beneficiary's canonical record has been created or updated
description: Domain-event-DDD payload published by AGG.REF.001.BEN.BENEFICIARY_IDENTITY on every state transition (REGISTER / UPDATE / ARCHIVE / RESTORE). Carries the FULL post-transition snapshot plus the monotonic `revision` field — consumers reconcile their local cache with last-write-wins on (internal_id, revision). The kind of transition is discriminated by `transition_kind`. Per ADR-TECH-STRAT-004, this event is the canonical replication path for downstream local-cache hydration.

event_id.description: Unique identifier of this resource event. Used by consumers for idempotency.

occurred_at.description: When the transition was committed in the producer.

transition_kind.description: Discriminator. Allows consumers to route without inferring from referential_status. See commands.yaml.transition_kinds.

internal_id.description: CORRELATION KEY. Canonical beneficiary identifier — immutable for the lifetime of the record (INV.BEN.002).

external_id.description: External reference set at registration; immutable thereafter.

revision.description: Monotonic revision counter. Consumers merge by last-write-wins on (internal_id, revision) and detect out-of-order delivery.

referential_status.description: Lifecycle status. PURGED is reserved for a future revision (see open question in README).

last_update_date.description: Timestamp of the most recent transition. Equal to occurred_at.

changed_fields.description: List of field names mutated by this transition. Populated for transition_kind=UPDATED; empty otherwise.

causation_command_id.description: command_id of the originating command (for traceability and audit).
